108.status: Has progressed to taking light swings and participating in light baseball activities.
Elbow soreness flared and required a stint on the IL. Not considered a serious injury and participated in light baseball activities on June 12.
Scheduled to start his throwing program in mid-June with a return date shortly after the All-Star break.

Made four rehab appearances before being shut down. MRI came back clean May 15. Rehabbing at the club's complex in Arizona and progressed to throwing off a mound on June 12, throwing only fastballs in a 10-pitch bullpen session.
Exited in the fifth inning on May 6 after injuring himself while trying to beat out an infield single. Progressed to not wearing the boot and playing catch on the field on June 10.
Underwent CT scan on June 10 that revealed the fracture and will undergo surgery that will keep him out roughly four weeks.

Said on June 7 his elbow had been bothering him his last three starts when throwing changeups. Underwent MRI exam on June 8 that revealed a torn ulnar collateral ligament that will require season-ending Tommy John surgery.

Participated in light on-field activity in early June but ultimately decided to undergo right knee surgery. He was placed on the 60-day IL on June 8, meaning he can't return until July 21 at the earliest.
Sustained injury playing with Double-A Rocket City on May 31. Added to 40-man roster because he had an opt-out in his Minor League contract and placed on IL.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Norfolk on June 11, collecting three strikeouts in a scoreless inning. Scheduled for another outing on June 13.
Continued to hit in cage during week of June 8. Expected to progress to live batting practice during week of June 15, then could begin rehab assignment shortly after.
Progressed to live batting practice during week of June 1. Threw a 35-pitch session of live BP on June 11 in Sarasota.
Placed on IL after departing June 3 start after three innings. Getting opinions on treatment, which could include medication, injection or rest.
Began throwing bullpen sessions the week of June 1.
Visited Dr. Keith Meister in Arlington, Texas, on May 19 and received a cortisone injection. Will begin throwing bullpen sessions the week of June 8.
Was evaluated on May 19 due to mild shoulder symptoms. Continuing to discuss treatment options. No clear timeline for return.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery on May 13. Expected to recover in time to return during the early part of the 2027 season.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.

111.status: Crochet said on June 12 he is "getting close" to throwing, something he hasn't been able to do since suffering a low-grade left lat strain on May 28.
Made second Minors rehab start on June 11, pitching 1 2/3 innings as he continues his build-up to a starter's workload.
Expected to resume swinging the week of June 15.
Continues to have right forearm tightness.
Is in the early stages of a hitting program, but not close to a return.
Given the projected 8-to-12 week timetable to return following his May 21 surgery, Story won't be eligible to be activated until just after the All-Star break.
Will soon start forearm strengthening exercises with an eye toward throwing by mid-June.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Making 75 throws out to 105 feet three days a week, and 30 throws at 60 feet three days a week while throwing changeups those days. Targeting mound work in late June.

112.status: Played catch and long toss on June 12 with no issues. Cubs will monitor Boyd in coming days with hope he can progress "rapidly," per manager Craig Counsell.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 3 prospect, No. 86 overall)
112.status: Last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Resumed throwing program in late April and has since advanced to throwing bullpen sessions and facing hitters in Arizona. A return to games could be coming soon.
Has resumed playing catch after feeling some discomfort in April 23 outing. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks.
Exited June 7 start after issuing a leadoff walk in the second inning, departing with training staff. Manager Craig Counsell said imaging showed a "moderate" strain.
Cleared to begin strengthening exercises on May 26.
Had setback (stress reaction near triceps/shoulder) after May 7 bullpen session, per manager Craig Counsell. Shut down for a month and moved rehab to Arizona.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

114.status: Threw bullpen sessions on June 8 and June 11. Feeling well. Team working to determine next step.

Started rehab assignment with Triple-A Columbus on June 4. Will build up while playing multiple positions. “We just want to have Gabby prepared for whatever the situation/role is,” manager Stephen Vogt said.

115.status: Hit his calf with foul ball on June 11. Scratched from starting lineup against the A’s on June 12, but was available to pinch-hit if needed.
Hit in the helmet by a 98.2 mph pitch from Milwaukee's Jacob Misiorowski on June 6. Should return when eligible on June 16, per manager Warren Schaeffer.
Set for pre-op appointment in mid-June. Looking like he may need UCL surgery, per manager Warren Schaeffer.More >
Manager Warren Schaeffer said that he can’t put a timetable on Moniak’s return. Minor League rehab assignment is still not scheduled.
Began rehab assignment with Triple-A Albuquerque on June 12. Could return during series against Cubs beginning on June 15.
Next rehab outing with Triple-A Albuquerque scheduled for June 13. Could return during series against Cubs beginning on June 15.
Has continued his throwing program since being scratched from a June 6 start, and expects to throw a full bullpen session soon.
Easy jogging at 90 feet and throwing at 90 feet "with some intent," on June 11 and scheduled to hit off a tee with a fungo bat. Will make the Las Vegas/Chicago trip.
Has been cleared to do some throwing on the field rather than merely in the batting cage, but he has not been scheduled beyond that. "If I'm outside, it's a good sign."

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continuing a throwing program at the team's complex in Scottsdale, Ariz., but has not been cleared to face hitters.
Receiving a second opinion on the UCL tear.
Has received a PRP shot in his elbow to promote healing.

115.playerName: Ethan Holliday (Rockies' No. 1 prospect, No. 15 overall)

Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
No return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

Threw his latest bullpen session on June 12. Not close to facing hitters yet.
Underwent a hydrodissection procedure on June 10 to address lingering soreness in elbow. Expected to resume playing catch next week.
Allowed four runs, all on solo homers, on five hits in six innings in his second rehab start with Triple-A Toledo on June 10 vs. St. Paul.
Will start June 13 at Cleveland, manager A.J. Hinch announced. Threw five scoreless innings with six strikeouts in a rehab start for High-A West Michigan on June 7.
Threw a bullpen session on June 9.
Transferred to 60-day IL on May 29. Saw a specialist and is shut down from throwing.

Retired all nine batters he faced, striking out three, in his second Minor League rehab start June 9, which came at Triple-A Sugar Land. He topped out at 94 mph and threw 44 pitches (29 strikes).
Expected to miss extended time after sustaining injury in June 8 win over Angels.
Started at catcher and went 1-for-3 with a run scored in his first Minor League rehab game June 9 at Triple-A Sugar Land.
Hopes to return to action when his 10 days are up. Injured in June 7 game against the A's.
Working out in in West Palm Beach, Fla., beginning therapeutic exercises.
Underwent peroneus brevis tendon repair surgery on his left ankle May 11. Will be out for the remainder of the 2026 season.
Has begun a throwing progression. Was scratched before his scheduled start against the Twins in Minneapolis on May 19.
Has started a throwing progression. He first experienced soreness during his throwing program on May 8 and was sent home to Houston to see doctors.

Sustained a setback with a shoulder issue arising after he began rehab assignment with Triple-A Omaha on June 9, allowing six runs (five earned) on eight hits in 1 1/3 innings. Will see a doctor this weekend to determine next steps.
Threw second bullpen session on June 12 after throwing 23 pitches in first bullpen on June 10. Will determine next steps on how he feels this weekend.
Suffered shoulder injury while rehabbing foot injury May 6. Began playing catch on May 27 and amped up his intensity and volume of a flat-ground throwing session June 12.
Diagnosed with Grade 3 tear of a portion of left plantar fasciitis, which is a severe tear. Timeline will be clearer over next few days after further evaluation.
Imaging done May 29 revealed inflammation in shoulder but nothing more severe. Began throwing progression week of June 8 and could progress to a bullpen by end of week.

Underwent another elbow surgery May 22 to try to help continued problems with soreness and inflammation. Return-to-play timeline is unknown so far and will depend on how McArthur recovers in the short-term.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

His back is beginning to feel better, so he could resume playing catch soon.
Joined Single-A Ontario to begin a rehab assignment on June 12. His timeline will depend on how his foot responds to the workload.

Began a throwing program on June 5, two and a half weeks after undergoing surgery.
Joined Triple-A Oklahoma to begin a rehab assignment on June 4. Will likely take the maximum 30 days to build up.
Working through a throwing program at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona, but he has had trouble ramping up.

Received promising MRI exam results on June 9 after pausing his rehab assignment three days earlier. Expects to restart rehab assignment exclusively as a DH early the week of June 14, if not sooner.
Began baseball activities in early June but remains on a fluid timeline depending on the progression of his symptoms. Expects to play again this season.
Fractured his thumb sliding into first base in a May 2 loss to the Angels. Will miss 6-8 weeks. Restarted baseball activities in early June.
Started running on June 4. Timetable will become more specific once he restarts baseball activities.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Was initially given a 4-8-week timeline but was still not throwing as of an early June update.

Fractured his leg on a comebacker May 15. Will need 6-8 weeks to heal and another six or so for a Spring Training-style build-up. That should take him well past the All-Star break in a best-case scenario.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.

Received cortisone shot on June 10. A's are hopeful he can return when first eligible on June 19.
MRI on June 1 revealed a strain of the shoulder capsule and subscapularis muscle. Will receive PRP injection on June 4 and be re-evaluated 4-6 weeks from that date.
Shut down from throwing for a minimum of three weeks, beginning May 18. Received an ultrasound guided cortisone injection on May 19.

Pulled from rehab assignment after sustaining a "high-grade" hamstring strain on May 19. Was scheduled to receive a platelet-rich plasma injection May 26 and will be reassessed after the All-Star break.
Underwent season-ending left hip surgery on May 19. Will rehab the rest of this season in hopes of being ready for 2027 Spring Training.

134.status: Began rehab assignment June 10 in Bradenton, Fla. Rehab transferred to Triple-A Indianapolis on June 12.

134.injury: Left hand fourth and fifth metacarpal non-displaced fractures
134.status: Recurrent left-hand discomfort after a hard slide in Atlanta over the weekend. Has been receiving treatment from the club’s medical staff. Diagnosed with non-displaced fractures.

Experienced elbow soreness, diagnosed with minor right forearm strain on May 27. Saw Dr. Keith Meister in Dallas on June 2. Began throwing plyometric balls on June 7. Began throwing from 90 feet on June 10.
Transferred from 15-day IL to 60-day on June 7 to make room for infielder Davis Wendzel on the 40-man roster. Began side progression the first week of June.

135.status: Continuing his recovery in Arizona but is not yet scheduled for a rehab assignment.
Experienced concussion symptoms after a fastball off the chin on April 18. Has resumed light baseball activity and is ramping up in Arizona.
Sidelined after an MRI revealed a flexor strain. Began playing catch in late May, and progressed to throwing from 90 feet, with some intensity, on June 10.
Began throwing program in late May. Pitched two simulated innings at Petco Park on June 9. Could return as a reliever.

Underwent surgery to repair a torn labrum in his right hip on June 5. First sustained the injury in 2021, but it recurred and worsened over the season's first two months. Likely to miss the remainder of the season.
Began experiencing knee soreness in late May, and landed on the IL when it worsened. Team is optimistic his absence won't be lengthy.
Began a Minor League rehab stint on June 2, pitching 2 1/3 scoreless innings for Triple-A El Paso.
Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early-season return but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Began playing catch in late May.
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.

136.status: Said on June 12 he does not expect to go on a Minors rehab assignment. Took ground balls and threw to all bases on June 11 for first time since injury. Has remained with club on road trip to rehab.More »
Homered again during his second rehab game at Triple-A Tacoma and third overall, after hitting two, including a grand slam, on June 9. Expected to remain with Rainiers through the week.More »

Traveled to Seattle on June 11, one day after being placed on the IL with the same injury that sidelined him 18 games earlier this season. Will soon transition rehab to club's Spring Training facility in Arizona.More »
Placed on IL one hour before June 9 game. Unclear how he sustained injury or how long he'll be sidelined.
Rehab assignment moved to Triple-A Tacoma after two games with High-A Everett.
Currently running on a weightless treadmill, and will be assessed for a running program in the next few days. Likely to return to Seattle for a check-in during the team's next road trip.More »
Undergoing an MRI on May 29 to make sure everything is clear before ramping up his throwing program.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Threw a simulated game on June 9. Scheduled to make a rehab start for Triple-A Sacramento on June 14.
Progressed to taking batting practice and will continue his running progression later this week.
Threw a bullpen session on June 9 at Oracle Park.
Saw Dr. Robert Anderson on June 5 and received a stem cell injection.
Pulled off his rehab assignment on June 5 due to right shoulder fatigue. Will be shut down for a few days before returning to pitch for Triple-A Sacramento this weekend.
Underwent surgery in Arizona on June 4 to remove the fractured hamate bone from his left wrist.
Hasn't played for Triple-A Sacramento since May 23 due to the wrist fracture; will be re-examined in two weeks.
Will miss two to three weeks with the adductor strain.
Sustained the injury fielding a ground ball between the mound and first base on May 31.
Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A Sacramento on May 30.

Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Had a setback during his rehab and is now dealing with a left elbow issue similar to the right elbow injury that sent him to the IL. He was transferred to the 60-day IL on June 12.
Fernandez woke up with back tightness and received an injection.

139.status: Signed to a Major League contract on April 14. Began a rehab assignment with two one-inning outings for the Rookie-level Florida Complex League Rays. Threw two scoreless innings for Triple-A Durham on June 2, June 6 and June 10.

Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture. Began a rehab assignment with the Rookie-level FCL Rays on June 11, going 2-for-3 with a steal, so still on track for a mid-June return to Triple-A.

Felt stiffness in wrist while playing catch on May 29. Resumed playing catch June 3, threw a bullpen session on June 6 and faced hitters in a 25-pitch live BP session on June 9. Next step to be determined, but should return when eligible June 12 or soon after.

Felt hamstring “grab” while running to first base on an infield single May 22. MRI revealed a Grade 1 strain. Faced Craig Kimbrel (and homered) in live BP at Tropicana Field on June 9. Expected to begin a rehab assignment on June 12.

Initially sidelined by a right shoulder injury, had his rehab assignment stalled by a sprained ankle on April 11, then shut down May 12 due to inflammation in his left shoulder. "Not close to games," per manager Kevin Cash, but had two at-bats against Craig Kimbrel in live BP on June 9.

Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8, 2025, with target return in June or July. Throwing bullpens and making progress, but still no decision on when he will progress to facing hitters.

Felt soreness during recent series in New York and while pitching in Baltimore on May 25. Imaging revealed mild tendon inflammation. Threw a 20-pitch bullpen on June 8 and is scheduled to face hitters on June 11.

Scratched from May 16 lineup and placed on IL May 17. Running, playing catch and in the early stages of a hitting progression three weeks after surgery. Manager Kevin Cash initially said he could require 6-8 weeks of recovery after the operation.

Reported soreness in back of right shoulder on April 15. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Had a setback while playing catch from 75 feet, so he underwent a PRP injection. "Going to be a while," manager Kevin Cash said.

After being designated for assignment on May 29 following an outing on May 27, Heasley was returned to the roster on June 2 due to an injury he suffered while active with the Rays and placed on the 15-day IL with no timeline for his return. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 8.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Also underwent an appendectomy. Playing catch with the goal to start bullpen sessions on June 8.

Underwent season-ending surgery on May 13. Multiple exams revealed "normal" wear and tear on labrum and femoroacetabular impingement, which limited his leg's range of motion. Expecting a six-month recovery followed by a full offseason to be ready for Spring Training.

140.status: The shortstop was held out of the lineup in Boston on June 12 after feeling soreness in his jaw and ribs, the result of a home plate collision suffered in Kansas City a day prior.
Last appeared in a June 9 loss to the Royals, giving up a go-head homer to Jac Caglianone.

Felt something in his arm after a June 1 start in St. Louis. Scans showed elbow inflammation and he will continue to be monitored over the next 10 days.
Threw a 20-pitch live batting practice session on June 3.

Martin missed 30 games (April 15 to May 19) previously. He allowed four earned runs in four appearances (four innings) since returning, surrendering seven hits and two walks without recording a strikeout.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23. Moved to 60-day IL on May 31.
Had a setback in his throwing program, but began throwing from 60 feet during the Rangers’ most recent homestand and has been cleared to begin his progression again.
Reached 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23 but experienced a setback and has been shut down.

141.status: Received a cortisone injection on June 11. Placed on 10-day injured list June 12, retroactive to June 10.
Is throwing and hitting at the team’s complex in Florida and hopes to start rehab games next week.
Will make one more rehab start with Triple-A Buffalo before joining the club.
Will pitch for Triple-A Buffalo on June 13 and could return to the Blue Jays soon after.
Began hitting progression in Dunedin, Fla.
Will be having a scope surgical procedure on his knee after receiving a second medical opinion. It is not believed to be season-ending.
Transferred to 60-day IL as recovery continues for right shoulder impingement.
Underwent full Tommy John surgery on May 20 and will miss 12 to 18 months.
Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Scheduled for a bullpen June 13. Next steps depend on how he responds, according to general manager Jeremy Zoll. Threw 47 pitches in three scoreless innings for Triple-A St. Paul on June 10.

Scheduled for MRI on June 12 after experiencing renewed symptoms. Scratched from scheduled rehab appearance June 5. Rehab shut down for now, due to a lack of recovery. Rules prohibit Sands from restarting rehab for seven days.
Alternating between DH and the outfield for Class-A Fort Myers. Went 0-for-1 with two walks on June 11.
Scheduled to throw live BP on June 13 in Fort Myers. If he responds well, a rehab assignment would be the next step of his recovery.

Has been running, throwing, lifting weights, performing catching drills and, as of June 10, taking dry swings. Underwent surgery on May 20. Next steps will be determined by how he tolerates his current workload.
Played catch on June 11 and June 12. Will need another week to build up arm strength before he begins throwing off a mound.
Scheduled for a followup with his surgeon on June 23 to assess his recovery from UCL surgery. Could be cleared to begin gripping exercises if progress continues.
Playing long toss, still 10 days from throwing off a mound.

143.status: Injured on June 10 at Toronto after attempting to throw out a runner at the plate. Awaiting one final opinion, but unlikely to return in 2026.
Experienced right elbow discomfort while ramping up baseball activities for return from 80-game suspension. Surgery with internal brace is required.
Scheduled to begin rehab assignment with Triple-A Lehigh Valley on June 9.

143.status: Underwent procedure on June 5 to address recurring back issue. Phillies hope he returns to Minor League game action in six to eight weeks.

Has started throwing bullpen sessions, has begun to face hitters in live batting practice as well.
Manager Walt Weiss said the strain is not believed to be as severe as the one that sidelined Acuña for 16 days in May..
Could be available in the second half of July. Was called for catcher's interference when Hyeseong Kim's bat hit his glove during May 10 game at Dodger Stadium.
Underwent arthroscopic surgery to remove bone spurs from his elbow on Feb. 18. Began throwing in early May and is up to 75 feet in his throwing progression.

Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.
Will miss the 2026 season after tearing his right ACL during a collision at first base during a Grapefruit League game on March 8.
Missed 2025 while recovering from left knee surgery and he had a cleanup procedure in November. There is no timetable for his return.

Teel is once again feeling great and hoping to get on an injury rehab assignment sooner than later. It’s a sentiment echoed by general manager Chris Getz.
Gilbert was replaced on the roster by fellow southpaw Chris Murphy.

Has felt discomfort -- off and on -- for the past five weeks, but it flared up while warming up on May 28. He will not throw for the next four weeks.
Recently underwent an appendectomy, but has resumed throwing. The procedure did delay his rehab from Tommy John surgery, though.
Had a setback in his rehab process likely pushing him into the second half of the season. His timeline is uncertain at this point.
Hicks had felt general soreness in the area for close to two weeks. But it grew increasingly worse, so the White Sox made the move.
First injury rehab outing with Rookie-level ACL White Sox on May 13 ended after four pitches out of the zone. Was returned from that rehab assignment due to right elbow inflammation.
White Sox Opening Day starter left his last start for Triple-A Charlotte on April 30 due to the shoulder issue. It’s the first time Smith has dealt with this malady this season.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.
Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.

146.status: Took boot off June 9 and has played some catch, according to manager Clayton McCullough. Club will have a better timeline this weekend. Had been dealing with soreness since May 9 and it hadn't gotten better.More >
Has been throwing at 90 feet, according to manager Clayton McCullough. Sustained the injury during stretching in between innings on May 27.
Estimated timeline for return was 12-16 weeks from date of injury.More >

Must get through multiple days in a row of high-intensity running before beginning rehab assignment, per manager Clayton McCullough. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 1. Underwent left hamstring tendon excision week of April 13.
Injured during warm-up drills on May 31, returned to Miami for treatment June 1. Timeline for return to play is approximately six weeks.
First felt the discomfort in mid-April in Atlanta but it never affected his pitching, just the build-up. Had trouble breathing leading up to testing. Transferred to 60-day IL on June 1.More >

146.injury: Left elbow UCL repair surgery with an internal brace
146.status: Underwent surgery on May 22 as recommended by Dr. Keith Meister. Recovery period calls for 10-12 months.
No surgery required but also no timeline for a return.

146.injury: UCL reconstruction with an internal brace (hybrid)
146.status: Underwent surgery, which has a recovery period of 13-14 months, on March 11. Experienced the discomfort on a pitch during his March 2 outing.

146.injury: Right elbow UCL reconstruction surgery with an associated internal brace
146.status: Underwent the hybrid procedure in December.

147.status: MRI showed healing, manager Aaron Boone said June 12. Expected to throw from mound June 13 for “touch and feel” session. Has been throwing at 120 feet and is said to be asymptomatic.
Played right field with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre for the first time this season (second in his career) on June 9. Will continue in Minors at least through June 13, manager Aaron Boone said.
Has resumed baseball activities as of June 12. Team is undecided on whether he will need to play rehab games before activation. MRI on neck on June 8 came back negative.

147.injury: Stress fracture of first rib on right side
147.status: After a period of rest and recovery, will be evaluated again in 4-6 weeks (approximately July 3-July 17) to determine level of healing and appropriate next steps.
Has been performing agility exercises and running lightly. “He’s continuing to rehab and get ready,” manager Aaron Boone said June 12.
Most recent mound session was 20 pitches as of June 2 and said he is “feeling great.” Could face hitters by the end of June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.
Placed on IL while pitching with Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre. Will not throw for at least three weeks.

158.status: Threw two scoreless innings in relief on June 9 before landing on the IL three days later. (last updated: June 12)
Threw 68 pitches in 3 2/3 innings in an Arizona Complex League game on June 9 and rejoined the Brewers in Milwaukee to decide if a Major League start is next. (last updated: June 12)

After throwing 73 pitches and issuing five walks in 2 2/3 innings in his latest Arizona Complex League start on June 8 "without physical complaint," according to the club, Priester was scheduled to pitch again on June 13 (five innings, 70-75 pitches). Now the Brewers have pulled the plug on that plan, recalling Priester from a rehab assignment for the second time. He will remain on the IL. (last updated: June 11)
Exited Brewers' June 4 game against the Giants. Manager Pat Murphy said Hall has a “return to play” timeline of four to six weeks. (last updated: June 9)
Originally was optioned to Triple-A Nashville, but that move was later reversed when he reported discomfort. (last updated: June 8)
Exited June 5 appearance warming up for the seventh inning. Will get second opinion before determining if Tommy John surgery is needed. (last updated: June 6)

Returned from the IL on May 31 only to feel discomfort during a pregame throwing session and land back on the IL. MRI scan showed trap strain. Will see a specialist to further pinpoint a diagnosis. (last updated: June 3)
Took live at-bats against rehabbing Brewers starter Brandon Woodruff on June 3 and is nearing a rehab assignment with Triple-A Nashville, manager Pat Murphy said. (last updated: June 3)
Received a facet injection in his back on June 2 to relieve inflammation. (last updated: June 3)
Threw eight of 10 pitches for strikes for Triple-A Nashville on May 29 in the first of what was expected to be a multi-outing rehab assignment. (last updated: May 30)
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery in Dallas on May 11. (last updated: May 12)
